text:
Sorry, this video isn't available any more.

Olympicswimmer Tom Dean, who clinched a gold medal inParis, uncomfortably laughed off his faux-pas after leaking his place on Strictly Come Dancing 2024 before the line-up was announced.

Despite weeks of rumours about the potential 2024 line-up as the BBC battles ongoing controversy around the alleged toxic atmosphere backstage, Tom, 24, became the first name to officially kick off the celebrity cast.

Albeit without meaning to since the BBC is yet to announce him as a contestant.

After making the final of the men’s 200 metres individual medley on Thursday, he said about Strictly: ‘Naturally, you want to take a break after you’ve had a full Olympic cycle and the opportunity arose.

‘I thought it would be really exciting to try something a bit different.’

Wake up to find news on your TV shows in your inbox every morning with Metro’s TV Newsletter.

Sign up to our newsletterand then select your show in the link we’ll send you so we can get TV news tailored to you.

Now he’s acknowledged the slip-up on Capital Breakfast on Tuesday as Tom went red and shook his head while the Strictly music played on the show.

‘Are we talking about this or not?’ presenter Chris Stark prodded, as the athlete stayed staunchly quiet on the topic.

‘I think I uh… am just focusing on enjoying the Olympic moment right now and spending a lot of time with my teammates, and I’ll see what the summer holds,’ he diplomatically responded.

‘As I say, Tom just says it how it is. And I think what’s hilarious is, I never think you’re meant to blab these things too early. You can get in loads of trouble for it so I think he’s just being super professional by not giving anything away on that,’ Chris added.

Jordan North then joked that he was also rumoured for the dance floor and could be going head to head with Tom before quickly confirming he was ‘absolutely not doing it.’

Last Thursday, however, Tom did give a peek into his post-Olympic plans, adding: ‘I’ll be doing [Strictly] and straight back into training after it. It’s something to look forward to. I’ve not seen [Adam] yet. He did brilliantly on it so I’ll be getting a few tips from him, for sure.’

Fellow GB swimmer Adam Peaty took part in the series in 2021 with pro partner Katya Jones where he finished ninth place.

The news comes after insiders reported that Tom – who also won two gold medals at the TokyoOlympics– was switching out the pool for the stage as part of this year’s BBCStrictly Come Dancingline-up.

‘It’s quite a coup for the producers to bag an Olympian fresh from winning gold in Paris, and swimmers have been shown to be great contestants,’ a sourcetold the Sun.

‘They tend to be a sensation as a result of their fitness, competitiveness and sheer determination to win — and Tom will still be pumped from his victory in France.’

The source added that although the Maidenhead swimmer doesn’t have quite the same profile as other top-level swimmers that would ‘drastically change’ after his stint on the hit reality series.

He joins a long legacy of elite athletes to appear in the series including Olympic gymnastClaudia Fragapane, Paralympic swimming medalistLauren Steadmanand bronze medalistLouis Smith.

Notably, Louis became Strictly champion in 2012 while Claudia advanced to the semi-finals in 2016 as did Lauren in 2018 meaning Tom could be in with a fighting chance to add to his accolades as part of the 2024 line-up.

If his fellow athlete’s successes are anything to go by.

The MBE recipient is the first to confirm but is far fromthe only public figure rumouredto be part of the 2024 line-up which will also celebrate the show’s 20th anniversary.

Other big names potentially in the mixinclude Hannah Waddingham, Pearl Mackie, Janet Street-Porter, Sarah Millican, GK Barry, Ralf Little and Jill Scott.

The line-up rumours come against the backdrop of increasing behind-the-scenes turbulence for the series.

Want to be the first to get all the latest news and rumours from the Strictly dancefloor?

Join ourStrictly Come Dancing WhatsApp channelto keep up to date with all the behind-the-scenes gossip about your favourite pros, judges and celebrities.

Simplyclick on this link, select ‘Join Chat’ and you’re in! Don't forget to turn on notifications so you can see when we've just dropped the latest spoilers. Keep dancing!

Over the past year, Strictly pros Giovanni Pernice andGraziano Di Primahave been axed from the show following concerning allegations from ex-dance partners such asAmanda AbbingtonandZara McDermott, respectively.

Giovanni hasdenied all allegationsand has vowed to clear his name, while Di Primasaid he ‘deeply regrets’ the events that led to his departurefrom the show.

Meanwhile,Love Island’s Tasha Ghouri, understood to have previously dated Giovanni, is also rumoured to be taking part in this year’s competition.

The reality star and dancer made history as the ITV show’s first-ever deaf contestant.

Discussing her potential inclusion, a source toldThe Sun: ‘Whilst fans who like a journey will be irked she is a professional dancer, others who enjoy watching incredible moments from the off will be more than satisfied.

‘She’s also become a powerful force for good campaigning and raising awareness for those with disabilities.

‘The Giovanni link is unfortunate to say the least, but above all else Tasha is a gorgeous girl who deserves this amazing opportunity.’

In 2023, actor Ellie Leach and her partner Vito Coppola secured the Glitterball trophy after a close final.

title: Olympic gold medallist awkwardly squirms in confrontation over Strictly 2024 ‘leak’ | Metro News
description: After Olympic champion Tom Dean leaked he is joining the Strictly Come Dancing 2024 line-up, he squirmed about the awkward situation on Capital.
